headline: Management That Matters: 2024’s Best U.S. Companies Unveiled
summary: The 2024 Drucker Institute Company Rankings identify the 30 best-managed U.S. companies based on corporate effectiveness across five categories: customer satisfaction, financial strength, social responsibility, innovation, and employee engagement. Apple secured the top spot as the best-managed company with an effectiveness score of 92.1, surpassing Microsoft from its 2023 position. Nvidia and Microsoft followed, with Intel and MasterCard rounding out the top five, demonstrating the importance of innovation and financial strength for high effectiveness scores.
label: negative

Key features include Writing Tools for text refinement, Live Translation, Image Playground for generating images and Genmoji, and an enhanced Siri with on-screen awareness and device knowledge, all while prioritizing user privacy through on-device processing and Private Cloud Compute. The system also seamlessly integrates ChatGPT for broader expertise.

The Apple Watch is presented as a tool for independence for children and peace of mind for parents.

headline: Nvidia dethroned as world's most valuable company after just a few days on top
summary: Nvidia's reign as the world's most valuable company was short-lived, with a two-day selloff erasing over $220 billion from its market capitalization and pushing it to third place behind Apple and Microsoft. Despite a 6.7% drop, analysts see no fundamental issues and expect any volatility to be temporary, reiterating "buy" ratings and raising price targets due to confidence in its AI leadership. The decline also coincided with broader market turbulence from a "triple-witching" options expiration.

summary: News Corporation has announced a new repurchase program to buy back up to US$1 billion of its Class A and Class B common stock. This buy-back will be conducted sporadically in the open market or through other methods, excluding ASX-listed CDIs. The program is conditional on market factors and the stock's trading price, with 161,397,541 securities on issue in the class to be bought back.

Broadcom's Hock Tan topped the list as the highest-paid CEO, earning over $161 million. This initiative aims to enhance campus experiences by providing seamless access to services via Apple and Google Wallet for students, faculty, and staff, aligning with the university's commitment to sustainability and modern student services. summary: J.M. Smucker's CFO, Tucker Marshall, notes that inflation is causing pet owners to reduce treat purchases and overall snacking, impacting the company's outlook despite strong growth in products like Uncrustables. The company achieved a 10% increase in quarterly profit per share, beating estimates, but faces challenges from rising coffee prices and reduced discretionary spending, leading to a revised lower sales forecast for the year.

Conversely, Qualcomm and Synaptics were downgraded due to increased competition and limited growth, while Intel, Micron, Marvell, and Monolithic Power Systems faced negative findings. Broadcom's stable outlook is tempered by potential future competition from Apple.

(NASDAQ: TNDM) has announced the commercial launch of its new Tandem Mobi, the world's smallest durable automated insulin delivery system, now shipping to eligible customers in the United States. This system integrates the Control-IQ advanced hybrid closed-loop technology, offering improved diabetes management and flexibility through its compact design, various wearability options, and compatibility with the Dexcom G6 Continuous Glucose Monitoring System, with future integrations planned.

headline: DOJ's Visa antitrust lawsuit alleges debit card company monopoly
summary: The Justice Department has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Visa, accusing the company of running a debit card monopoly that imposed billions of dollars in additional fees on American consumers and businesses. The lawsuit claims Visa stifles competition and charges excessive fees by controlling over 60% of U.S. debit transactions. Visa denies the allegations, stating its network offers security and value.

headline: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway is buying up shares of a lagging 1990s dotcom darling
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested $74 million in VeriSign, despite the infrastructure company's stock falling 2% during a strong year for tech. Berkshire increased its stake by buying approximately 377,736 shares, making it VeriSign's largest shareholder with over 13 million shares worth about $2.6 billion. The investment is notable given VeriSign's high profit and operating margins and comes as Berkshire has been accumulating cash and selling off other stock holdings.

These strategies include memorizing your company's story, embracing risky live demos, rethinking traditional 1:1 meetings, and leveraging advisors over extensive outbound sales. Lerman emphasizes the importance of direct customer engagement, strategic board appointments, and prioritizing product-market fit above all else, while encouraging founders to have fun and construct their company their own way.

headline: When $2B facility opens, Amkor will become Peoria's largest private employer
summary: Amkor Technology Inc.'s planned $2 billion semiconductor packaging and testing plant in Peoria, recently greenlit by the City Council, is poised to become the city's largest private employer. This mega-project will bring 2,000 new jobs to the Phoenix metro area and significantly bolster the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, with the facility packaging and testing chips for major companies like TSMC and Apple. Amkor, a leading provider in outsourced semiconductor services, had $6.5 billion in annual sales in 2023 and employs 30,000 worldwide.

summary: Amazon Prime Day 2024 is concluding in the early hours of Wednesday and Thursday, depending on the time zone, giving shoppers a final chance to snag deals on various products. The two-day event, which started Tuesday, features discounts on Amazon services and devices, as well as popular items like Apple AirPods, iRobot Roomba, and Renpho Eye Massagers. The sales are exclusive to Prime members, though non-members can sign up for a free 30-day trial to participate.

Investors are concerned that increased capital spending will negatively affect cash flows, especially as the company has historically performed better when focused on profitability. This shift to investment mode, coupled with Amazon's lack of a dividend and less aggressive share buyback program compared to competitors, is causing investors to pause on the stock.

headline: How Boston Scientific's digital and IT boss upgraded her team from order takers to strategic thinkers
summary: Jodi Eddy, Boston Scientific's SVP and chief information and digital officer, transformed her IT team from order-takers to strategic thinkers, enabling technology to address complex healthcare systems. headline: Ford most at risk from China exposure among 250 American companies, consulting firm says

Ford scored 69 out of 100 on their exposure index, surpassing Apple and Tesla, due to factors like revenue in China, partnerships, and supply chain risks. Ford declined to comment on the rankings, citing a lack of detailed methodology.

headline: Cirrus Logic Celebrates 40 Years of Innovation and Excellence

The company has a rich history of developing groundbreaking solutions for audio and electronics industries, holding over 3,900 patents, and has grown through strategic refocuses and acquisitions like Wolfson Microelectronics. CEO John Forsyth and VP Carl Alberty emphasize the company's commitment to innovation, customer trust, and its award-winning culture that fosters employee loyalty and professional growth.

headline: How Corning is ushering in the AI revolution
summary: Corning, a 173-year-old glass-making company, is playing a crucial role in the AI revolution by producing next-generation fiber optic cables that power data centers for tech giants. Under CEO Wendell Weeks, the company has seen its market cap reach $41 billion and 2023 revenue hit $13 billion, with optical fiber now contributing 30% of its revenue. Weeks' leadership and innovative approach, admired by figures like Jeff Bezos and Jony Ive, are key to Corning's success in this evolving technological landscape.

headline: Goldman Sachs top strategist: We’re on the precipice of a summer correction
summary: Goldman Sachs's tactical strategist Scott Rubner warns of an impending summer correction for the S&P 500, citing historical data indicating a downturn typically begins around July 17th, followed by weak August outflows. He believes the "pain trade is no longer higher" as good news is already priced in, and systematic funds have reached maximum length. Rubner recommends put options on the Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 to clients.

summary: IBM has reportedly hit its highest stock price since 2013, nearing an all-time high, driven by investor optimism in its AI and cloud investments. Despite trailing Magnificent Seven stocks in five-year returns, IBM's 19% gain this year, fueled by strong Q4 2023 results and AI demand, positions it for potential re-rating. Wall Street remains divided on its growth prospects, but its robust 3.43% dividend yield sets it apart from many big tech companies.

headline: Apple supplier Jabil benches its CEO as it investigates his conduct—but he’ll still collect his full $1 million salary

The company's CFO, Michael Dastoor, has been appointed interim CEO as the probe, which is unrelated to financial statements, unfolds. This comes less than a year after Wilson took the helm, following a long career within the company.

headline: Government sues Ticketmaster owner and asks court to break up company's monopoly on live events
summary: The Justice Department and 30 states have filed a sweeping antitrust lawsuit against Ticketmaster and its parent company, Live Nation Entertainment, accusing them of operating an illegal monopoly over live events. The lawsuit seeks to break up the company, alleging that its practices stifle competition, harm artists, and inflate ticket prices for consumers through excessive fees. Live Nation denies the allegations, stating that the lawsuit will not address the real issues affecting ticket prices and that competition has eroded Ticketmaster's market share.

headline: Diversification: Definition, How It Works
summary: This article defines diversification as an investment strategy that spreads investment dollars across various assets to reduce risk and enhance potential returns. It explains how diversification minimizes the impact of market volatility and asset-specific risks by blending dissimilar assets like stocks and bonds across different industries, geographies, and company sizes. The piece also discusses methods for achieving diversification, including asset allocation, mutual funds, ETFs, target-date funds, and robo-advisors.

headline: Apple class action allege App Store offers malicious crypto apps
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple Inc., claiming the company authorized and maintained malicious crypto applications on its App Store, leading to the theft of consumers' personal financial assets. Plaintiffs allege Apple misrepresented the safety and legality of these apps, including Digicoins, SolLuna, and Forex5, which they assert were "spoofing" programs designed to steal account information. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers for alleged violations of California's Consumers Legal Remedies Act and Unfair Competition Law.
label: neutral

summary: Large-cap tech stocks, particularly the Magnificent Seven, are expected to continue their dominance and strong performance in 2025, according to market observers. ETFs like Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) offer efficient exposure to these high-performing companies for growth-focused portfolios.

headline: Growth ETFs Shaken Up as Tech Takes on Value Blush
summary: Growth ETFs like IVW are undergoing significant rebalancing as major tech companies such as Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Adobe are no longer considered pure growth stocks by S&P Global. This reclassification means investors will see a smaller allocation to these tech giants in growth ETFs and a larger presence of financial firms like Berkshire Hathaway and JPMorgan Chase, which are now being given a "growth blush". The rebalancing also shifts tech's share in the S&P Value Index significantly, with these companies now having their weightings divided between growth and value characteristics based on S&P's multifactor analysis.
label: positive

headline: Why Samsung and LG may be the reason Apple delaying the all-screen iPhone
summary: Apple's plan for an all-screen iPhone has been delayed past 2026, reportedly due to technical challenges faced by display manufacturers Samsung Display and LG Display. They are struggling with developing thin film encapsulation for OLEDs and suitable optical clear adhesives, pushing the potential release to 2027 or later. Apple aims for a bezel-free design that avoids the "magnifying glass effect" seen in other curved-screen phones.
label: neutral

headline: Apple’s been on an uncommonly strong run. So a tough January could now be in the cards.
summary: Apple's stock has achieved a rare five-week streak of 2%-plus gains, a phenomenon that historically precedes a roughly 5% drop in the following month. Investors are advised to be cautious as this pattern suggests a potentially tough January for Apple shares. The stock's recent performance marks the first time in 14 years and very rarely in the company's 44-year public history that it has had such a strong, sustained run.
label: negative

headline: Evolving the device experience at Microsoft
summary: Microsoft is transitioning its employee device management from a traditional on-premises model to a modern, cloud-based approach using Microsoft Intune, Azure Active Directory, and co-management strategies. This shift supports hybrid work, enables employees to be productive from anywhere on various devices (Windows, Android, iOS, macOS), and enhances security through Zero Trust principles and verified device health. The migration addresses challenges by using Hybrid Azure AD joined devices as a bridge and standardizing management practices.
label: positive
